content.full_text:
CITY OF FAIRFIELD
1000 WEBSTER STREET
FAIRFIELD, CA 94533
[707] 428-7419

Incorporated December 12, 1903

October 16, 1990

John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Request for Informal Assistance
Your File No. I-90-251

Dear Mr. Wallace:

Thank you for your letter of September 4, 1990. I have several questions about your
conclusions:

1. In the Burnham advice letter (Advice Letter No. A-84-062), the FPPC
concluded that a membership in a private, for profit club was a form of
"personal property". Relying on Burnham and Vickers (Advice Letter No.
I-89-575), I had believed that Mr. Bodutch's membership in the Rancho
Solano Resort and Racquet Club was an "asset". Your letter to me does not
mention Burnham; did you intend to distinguish Burnham or overrule its
conclusions?

2. In the Maddy advice letter (Advice Letter No. A-87-075), the FPPC
concluded that a membership in a racquet club (presumably for profit)
which consists only of user privileges and the right to sell the membership
is not an "investment" subject to the disclosure and disqualification
provisions of the Political Reform Act. Your letter to me does not mention
Maddy; as I have indicated by phone and letter, Mr. Bodutch's
membership in the Fairfield club consists only of user privileges and the
right to sell the membership. Did you intend to distinguish Maddy or
overrule its conclusions?

3. The distinction between Mr. Vickers' club and Mr. Bodutch's club is that
the former is non-profit and the latter is for profit. You seem to base your
conclusion that Mr. Vickers' membership is an asset and Mr. Bodutch's

membership is an investment on this distinction. However, I do not find any basis for that distinction in the definition of "investment" at Section 82034. In fact we know that a business entity includes a non-profit entity from Section 18700. of the Regulations. Please explain to me how the definition of "investment" supports the distinction you have made when Mr. Bodutch has no more "financial interest in" the Rancho Solano Resort and Racquet Club than Mr. Vickers' has in his non-profit club. Section 82034 requires an "ownership" interest in a business entity in order to have an "investment" in that business entity. Mr. Bodutch does not have any such ownership interest.

Thank you for your attention. I look forward to your reply.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Betsy Strauss
City Attorney

c: Jay Bodutch
Diane Fishburn
